title: pinus hartwegii subsp. donnell-smithii
intro: Pinus hartwegii subsp. donnell-smithii, also known as the Hartweg's pine or Donnell Smith's pine, is a subspecies of pine tree native to Mexico and Central America. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds found in its bark, leaves, and seeds. The health benefits of Pinus hartwegii subsp. donnell-smithii include reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator properties. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
